rule: Executable source and exact-head evidence outrank prose. Historical evidence remains preserved but does not provide current implementation instructions.

active_doctrine.purpose: Shared visual foundations and design tokens.
Employee interface behavior and operating primitives.
Validation, accessibility, browser and evidence requirements.
Ratified product standard; UI documents cannot silently amend it.

active_architecture_and_workflow.purpose: Active UX router and reading order.
Current UI surface, adapter, workbench and evidence topology.
Canonical UI Lab architecture, preset lifecycle and effects.
Cross-system Web, materialization and embedded-view boundary.
Fixture versus production evidence and environment policy.

informative_dated_or_specialized.disposition: Dated runtime review; valid facts remain informative, but current source and UX maps supersede topology/status.
Runtime-derived constraints; does not define current Web presentation architecture.
Heartbeat and fixture-state constraints; not the general UI Lab workflow.
Coverage audit; exact source and current validation artifacts determine present status.
Generated/embedded-view frontier, separate from employee presentation presets.
Controls speculative methods and non-release-path ideas.
Hermes integration findings; current AMTECH source remains product authority.
Implementation ledger; cannot redefine doctrine or exact current source.

historical_or_superseded.reason: Historical collaborator packets, screenshots, data catalogs and experiments.
Earlier design exploration; current port, strategy and UI Lab source supersede fixed visual assumptions.
Durable chronological handoffs, not active instructions.
Planning and implementation history unless explicitly routed by current authority.
Legacy parallel fixture client isolated from active routes; retained only until deletion is separately justified.

superseded_concepts: one fixed visual identity for every employee
one React page fork per employee type
themes or layouts described as adapters
UI Lab as a direct in-page fixture renderer without an isolated canvas
browser localStorage as the durable UI version store
production rebuild required for each visual edit
screenshots treated as interaction, runtime or production proof
dirty uncommitted designs assignable to customer employees
single black-shell or ink/white/red-only visual requirement
